mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS trend tightens discount rates and reduces equity duration values; higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS breaks materially above its recent trend, stronger consumer demand raises near-term revenue expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 2%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the return difference has t-test p > 0.05 in walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in UNRATE signals rising recession risk and weaker future earnings; when unemployment rises enough over three months, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if downside hit-rate is not above baseline in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in FEDFUNDS and fall in HOUST indicates policy-sensitive housing demand is weakening under tighter financing; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive and HOUST 3-month change is below -5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has no lower 20-day return mean than either single-series trigger alone, or if interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend signals improving production, utilization, and operating leverage; higher 6-month INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month INDPRO momentum has negative or zero Rank IC with 60-day S&P 500 returns across rolling 3-year windows, or if positive momentum does not outperform negative momentum.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL paired with rising FEDFUNDS tightens real financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS joint trigger is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction term is not negative in expanding-window tests.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with stable or falling UNRATE signals broad labor income support without immediate recession stress; this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 300 thousand over 3 months and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to deliver a positive average 5-day return or if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not outperform PAYEMS-only and UNRATE-only signals.

mechanism_narrative: High HOUST levels signal resilient residential investment and durable-goods demand; when housing starts cross a high expansion threshold, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST readings above 1600 are followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than readings below 1600, or if the triggered hit-rate is not above 50% in walk-forward testing.
